summary: Regression test for Attention/Urgency/Priority unification (Sprint 1) PASSED. Backend attention computation verified with all 20 tests passing. Frontend Pipeline page and Coach Mission Control verified working. computeAttention.js confirmed NOT imported at runtime.

minor.issue: owner field can be 'shared' in addition to athlete/coach/director - this is valid behavior, test updated

critical_code_review_comments: services/attention.py: VERIFIED - Returns all required fields: attentionScore, tier, attentionLevel, heroEligible, urgency, momentum, primaryAction, reason, ctaLabel, riskContext, flags, prioritySource, recapRank, owner
routers/athlete_dashboard.py: VERIFIED - Line ~674-700: enriches programs with attention field from services/attention.py
frontend/PipelinePage.js: VERIFIED - Line ~189-191: uses backend attention field directly (allAttention = allPrograms.filter(p => p.attention)...). No computeAllAttention() call
computeAttention.js: VERIFIED - NOT imported anywhere at runtime (grep confirms no imports)
PipelineHero.js: VERIFIED - Uses heroItem.program?.university_name, heroItem.attentionLevel, heroItem.primaryAction from backend
KanbanBoard.js: VERIFIED - Uses attn.attentionLevel for STATUS color, attn.owner for label
PriorityBoard.js: VERIFIED - Uses tier for rank, primaryAction for action text, riskContext for urgency_note
MomentumInsight.js: VERIFIED - Counts high-tier items correctly

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Pipeline page loads, hero card shows TOP PRIORITY badge, priority/kanban views work, coach mission control verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Attention SSOT refactor verified. Backend services/attention.py is canonical source. GET /api/athlete/programs returns attention field with ALL required fields. Frontend PipelinePage.js consumes backend attention directly without recomputation. computeAttention.js exists but is NOT imported anywhere. Coach mission control shows journey_state and attention_status correctly. Athlete has 6 programs. Hero card shows highest-scored school (UCLA). Kanban shows colored badges based on attentionLevel.

frontend_tests: Pipeline page loads - PASS: recruiting-board, pipeline-header, view-toggle found
Hero card - PASS: TOP PRIORITY badge, school name (UCLA), CTA button, descriptive reason
Priority Board - PASS: 2 urgency groups (Needs attention now, On track), 5 priority cards
Momentum Insight - PASS: Shows 'UCLA needs immediate attention — 5 others need follow-up'
Kanban view - PASS: 5 columns (Added, Outreach, Talking, Visit, Offered), 6 cards with attention badges
Page reload determinism - PASS: Board loads correctly after reload
Coach Mission Control - PASS: Shows athletes with journey_state and attention_status (CRITICAL, Worsening, Improving)

attention_SSOT.required_fields: attentionScore, tier, attentionLevel, heroEligible, urgency, momentum, primaryAction, reason, ctaLabel, riskContext, flags, prioritySource, recapRank, owner
attention_SSOT.athlete_dashboard: GET /api/athlete/programs enriches programs with attention field
attention_SSOT.frontend_consumption: PipelinePage.js reads p.attention directly, no recomputation

no_stale_imports.computeAttention.js: File exists but NOT imported anywhere at runtime (grep verified)

frontend_components.PipelineHero: Shows TOP PRIORITY badge, school name, primaryAction, heroReason/riskContext
frontend_components.PriorityBoard: Shows sorted list with rank labels (Needs attention now / On track), action text, urgency_note
frontend_components.KanbanBoard: Shows cards with attention badges/colors matching tier (red for high)
frontend_components.MomentumInsight: Shows correct count of high-priority schools

coach_mission_control.roster: Shows athletes with journey_state and attention_status
